Title: Glc-TOR signalling leads transcriptome reprogramming and meristem activation

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: PIKK family Ser/Thr protein kinase; TOR kinase
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: supports energy and biomass production for meristem activation and growth
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: biosynthesis, central carbon metabolism, secondary metabolism, cell cycle entry
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: phosphorylation activation of downstream effectors and transcriptional reprogramming
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: E2Fa; S6K1; genes in glycolysis, TCA cycle, ribosome biogenesis, amino acid/lipid/nucleotide biosynthesis, glutathione and glucosinolate pathways
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: activates broad anabolic and cell-cycle gene sets; represses catabolic, β-oxidation, glyoxylate-cycle, autophagy and some sugar-transporter genes
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: master nutrient-responsive controller linking glucose availability to anabolic transcriptome reprogramming and meristem activation
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: glucose; photosynthesis-derived glucose; glycolysis; mitochondrial electron transport/oxidative phosphorylation
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: functions as TOR kinase complex resembling rapamycin-sensitive mTORC1-like activity
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 15 mM glucose; light/CO2-enhanced photosynthesis; rapamycin; torin1; 2-DG; antimycin A; DCMU
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: inducible tor RNAi mutant; rapamycin inhibition; torin1 inhibition; S6K1 T449 phosphorylation assay; microarray; qRT-PCR; EdU staining; root meristem phenotyping; in vitro kinase assay
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: required for glucose-driven root meristem activation, root growth, and activation of broad biosynthetic gene networks
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: active at photoautotrophic transition in seedlings/root meristem under glucose supply
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: positive feedforward suggested by activation of glycolysis, TCA cycle and mitochondrial genes that reinforce TOR-mediated growth metabolism
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: TOR inhibition blocks meristem activation and growth
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Glucose activates TOR through glycolysis and mitochondrial energy relays. 2. TOR is essential for rapid transcriptome reprogramming that activates anabolic and cell-cycle genes and represses catabolic programs. 3. TOR directly phosphorylates E2Fa to promote S-phase gene expression and root meristem activation.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper emphasizes TOR as a central nutrient signalling regulator rather than a single-metabolite controller; metabolite effects are broad and transcriptionally coordinated.

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: E2F family transcription factor; DNA-binding/dimerization domain; N-terminal TOR-phosphorylated regulatory region
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: enables proliferative growth supported by nutrient-driven biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: cell-cycle transcription coupled to nutrient-responsive biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: ORC2; ORC6; MCM3; MCM5; MCM7; CDC6; ETG1; PCNA1
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: key downstream effector translating glucose-TOR signalling into S-phase gene induction and meristem activation
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: glucose; sucrose; rapamycin; torin1; antimycin A; 2-DG
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: e2fa null mutant; transient expression in protoplasts; ChIP-qPCR; in vitro TOR kinase assay; co-immunoprecipitation; qRT-PCR; EdU staining; root meristem phenotyping
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: promotes glucose-induced S-phase gene expression, root meristem expansion and root growth
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: function assayed in root meristem and leaf protoplasts
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: e2fa mutation compromises glucose-promoted root growth and meristem activation
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. E2Fa target genes substantially overlap with glucose-TOR-activated S-phase genes. 2. TOR directly phosphorylates the N-terminal regulatory region of E2Fa. 3. e2fa mutants show reduced glucose sensitivity in S-phase gene activation and impaired root meristem activation.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Acts downstream of TOR in a noncanonical route that bypasses or acts downstream of the classical CYC-CDK-RBR cascade.
